title: [BUG] `editAs` not working
author: EugeniyKiyashko
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

When you insert an image into a worksheet, the `editAs` property controls how the image is anchored to a cell(s). The default value of editAs is `oneCell`. It could be undefined, `oneCell` or `absolute` - [https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/master/README.md#add-image-over-a-range.](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/master/README.md#add-image-over-a-range.) But when you set it to `undefined`, the value of `editAs` in a generated excel file will still be `oneCell`.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15689080/92871200-579ed280-f40d-11ea-9330-e0dad705106c.png)

According to the official Excel documentation, valid values of `editAs` are the following: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-gb/dotnet/api/documentformat.openxml.drawing.spreadsheet.editasvalues?view=openxml-2.8.1. It looks like the documentation is not completely correct, and one of the acceptable values ('twoCell') is missing.

Lib version: 4.1.1

## Steps To Reproduce

Set editAs to `undefined`, but it is still `oneCell`.
```javascript
ws.addImage(imageId, {
  tl: { col: 2, row: 2 },
  br: { col: 3, row: 3 },
  editAs: undefined
});
```

## The expected behaviour:

Move and Resize With Anchor Cells. When the item is serialized out as xml, its value is "twoCell".

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/15689080/92871958-27a3ff00-f40e-11ea-9463-08b3ddeef920.png)


## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

```javascript
ws.addImage(imageId, {
  tl: { col: 2, row: 2 },
  br: { col: 3, row: 3 },
  editAs: 'twoCell'
});
```

comments.author: shahsaab
comments.body:
Setting the value as 'twoCell' works.

Not seeing any errors while opening the file, the image works with filters too.
But, does not move while sorting rows.

comments.author: wattonchd
comments.body:
` tl: { col: 0.2, row: r - 1 },  
  br: { col: 0.67, row: r },  
  editAs: 'xxx', `  
  'xxx'  is fine too, haha
Anything except ’oneCell‘ and ’absolute‘ and ’‘.

comments.author: nnfans
comments.body: is there any update regarding this issue?
